title: Unable to execute "podman ps" as non-privileged user
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A `podman ps` as a non-privileged user leads to `Error: cannot read pause process pid file /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: open /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: permission denied`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install the current PPA version (`podman version 1.3.2-dev`) on `18.04.2 LTS (Bionic Beaver)`
2. Execute `podman ps` as non-privileged user

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: cannot read pause process pid file /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: open /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: permission denied`

**Describe the results you expected:**

List of all running containers.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.3.2-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

As `root`:

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.3.2-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0-dev
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version , commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 285392896
  MemTotal: 2039857152
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: cjw246f3c000e02s65ya8h4g2
  kernel: 4.15.0-50-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 35m 29.87s
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @giuseppe @mheon Is this podman being confused about whether it is in rootless mode?

This is definitely rootless code. It doesn't seem to have found the correct
PID for Conmon to join the rootless user namespace.

On Sat, May 25, 2019, 06:14 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @giuseppe <https://github.com/giuseppe> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>
> Is this podman being confused about whether it is in rootless mode?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3201?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCD3H254JU3IBMTR2QTPXEGPXA5CNFSM4HPPZZ3KYY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGODWHN63A#issuecomment-495902572>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCAVLUJFJYHFFBQNP6LPXEGPXANCNFSM4HPPZZ3A>
> .
>

Well reading it looks like it is looking for the wrong UID then.
A podman ps as a non-privileged user leads to Error: cannot read pause process pid file /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: open /run/user/0/libpod/pause.pid: permission denied

While it should be looking for 
/run/user/USERUID/libpod/pause.pid
How does the `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` look like?  Podman would not use that directory if it is not writable, unless the environment variable is explicitly set

@giuseppe Thanks for the hint :slightly_smiling_face: The following works:

As "unprivileged user":

```sh
mkdir $HOME/run
export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=$HOME/run

podman ps
```

Would that the way to go? :slightly_smiling_face:

The question is how do you get this screwed up in the first place.  If you are logged in as non-root and execute podman it will use XDG_RUNTIME_DIR.  To me this looks like you logged in as root and then sudo to a non root user?  Or something like that.

If you login straight as @akoenig XDG_RUNTIME_DIR should be pointing to /run/user/AKOENIGUID.

$ printenv XDG_RUNTIME_DIR
/run/user/3267

$ id -u
3267

@rhatdan Well, your assumption is correct. I have an `cloud-init` script which starts the container via `sudo -u <user> bash -c 'podman run -d -t ...'`.

So in my case, I have to use the "workaround" I've described above (I guess).
If you have to make a run directory, please make it on a tmpfs - `/tmp` or `/var/run` or similar - Podman needs that directory to clear after a reboot to function properly
@mheon Thanks for the hint. I just got bitten by this scenario :slightly_smiling_face:
Yep, thanks again for the help 🙂
